summary:
The provided evidence identifies well-known risks associated with radiating tissue-expandable implants but finds no direct studies linking partial expander deflation to increased adverse effects from radiotherapy. Due to a lack of specific research addressing this interaction, it remains uncertain whether partial deflation aggravates treatment-related complications.  

\boxed{C}
